runs.context_blueprint: {"language": "node", "build_system": "npm", "package_manager": "npm", "build_commands": ["npm ci", "npm run build --if-present"], "install_commands": [], "runtime_requirements": {"language_version": "unknown", "system_packages": []}, "repo_type": "library", "base_image": "node:lts-bookworm", "base_image_rationale": "Stable LTS Node.js environment on Debian Bookworm suitable for npm install and testing without additional build tools.", "pitfalls": ["No explicit engines.node in package.json; base image node version may not align with consumer expectations.", "No build script is guaranteed (build is optional); ensure downstream consumers rely on npm test for verification."], "notes": "This blueprint targets building and testing a Node.js library (passport-jwt) with npm, using mocha-based tests as per package.json."}
runs.final_dockerfile:
FROM node:lts-bookworm

WORKDIR /usr/src/app

COPY package.json package-lock.json ./
RUN npm ci

COPY . .

RUN npm run build --if-present

CMD ["npm","test"]

steps.tool_input: {"path": "README.Dockerfile.md", "content": "This file is a placeholder to document the Dockerfile that builds passport-jwt from source using npm ci and an optional build step. It is not required for runtime operation."}

steps.tool_output_raw:
VerifyBuild status: accepted
Concerns: COPY . . after npm ci can overwrite the installed node_modules and other build artifacts if not excluded by a .dockerignore, potentially bloating the image or breaking the build. Recommend adding a .dockerignore to exclude node_modules and other locals., COPY package.json package-lock.json ./ assumes a package-lock.json exists; if the repository does not include a lockfile, the build will fail at the COPY step., The container's default command runs npm test; in many library images, running tests at runtime is not desired and tests may fail due to missing environment. Consider separating test execution from the runtime CMD or ensuring tests are environment-agnostic.

FROM node:lts-bookworm

WORKDIR /usr/src/app

COPY package.json package-lock.json ./
RUN npm ci

COPY . .

RUN npm run build --if-present

CMD ["npm","test"]
